what_it_does: This file is a living noticeboard guide that points to two stored documents—Tim's interface-synthesis verbatim and CODES_OF_CONDUCT v1—and summarizes their central concepts, then instructs readers to read both and respond with lively, unprompted questions and offers.
observations: Front matter declares type guide, state living, source claude_code, author_session ch-al7jdfdr, and a sourced_from link to board://item-0af8d61e.
Body references external files channel-memory/TIM-INTERFACE-SYNTHESIS-2026-06-16.md and channel-memory/CODES_OF_CONDUCT.md rather than quoting them.
Defines THE INTERFACE as a generative composition of three streams: wildcard, DNA, and composition.
Wildcard stream covers principles/backend/context and interaction MODES (slide/graph/document) as ideas, not code, with AI embedded in each part rather than as a side chat.
DNA stream covers language/presentation/voice/IDENTITY plus a GALLERY that is a hybrid presentation+UI fed by DATA FILES and meant to produce 'something new'.
Composition stream treats everything as slots/sockets/behaviour-as-DATA entered into a registry.
Frames the result as 'JUST ANOTHER PROJECTION': AI produces content+meaning, DNA resolves it to presented form, and it renders.
Introduces RIGOROUS RESPONSIBILITY: each member owns a SEGMENT, checks compatibility within their domain, and advocates/speaks up unprompted in the channel.
Announces CODES_OF_CONDUCT v1 covering roles/responsibilities/laws/protocols and explicitly invites people to 'ATTACK + shape it'.
Directs the task system to investigate the company's chains/graphs and agent/task tools and to build tracking from there.
Ends with Tim's instruction to read and chime in lively, unprompted, with questions and offers.
standouts: 'AI-in-each-part not chat-on-side' as a core interface stance.
'JUST ANOTHER PROJECTION' framing that separates meaning generation from rendering.
'ATTACK + shape it' invitation applied to a governance draft.
'RIGOROUS RESPONSIBILITY' requiring unprompted advocacy and segment ownership.
conventions: Follows the repository's YAML-frontmatter noticeboard-item schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, links, timestamps, and history.
Uses board:// URI addressing and sourced_from link kind.
Uses uppercase phrases as conceptual emphasis (e.g., THE INTERFACE, RIGOROUS RESPONSIBILITY, JUST ANOTHER PROJECTION).
Functions as an index/pointer to fuller documents rather than a self-contained specification.
Breaks typical passive documentation style by being an imperative invitation to critique and act.
concerns: The actual design and governance content lives in two external files, so this item is not independently verifiable.
Dense coined terms (wildcard, DNA, composition, registry, SEGMENT, GALLERY) are used without definition here.
'ATTACK' is colorful but procedurally ambiguous—unclear what constructive challenge looks like.
No acceptance criteria, deadline, or decision process for the governance draft is stated.
The call for 'lively, unprompted' participation provides no mechanism for accountability or visibility.
notes: A high-energy noticeboard pointer that advertises a design synthesis and a conduct draft, and tries to convert readers into active critics and owners.
questions: What exactly do the referenced TIM-INTERFACE-SYNTHESIS and CODES_OF_CONDUCT files contain?
What is the registry, and how do slots/sockets/behaviour-as-DATA map to implementation?
Who are the 'members', what are their SEGMENTS, and how is ownership assigned?
What does 'ATTACK + shape it' mean as a process—comments, edits, votes, or something else?
Which chains/graphs/agent/task tools should be investigated, and what tracking is expected?
How is 'unprompted' advocacy distinguished from normal participation?
What is the relationship between the three streams and any existing codebase or UI?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to share Tim's synthesis and the conduct draft so people read and chime in; the file does exactly that, while also embedding a compressed design manifesto, so it is slightly more prescriptive than a pure announcement.
apparent_themes: AI embedded throughout the system rather than bolted on as chat
Projection/rendering pipeline separating meaning from form
intention_signals: It appears designed to bootstrap a shared design vocabulary and a culture of active co-ownership by making a synthesis and a governance draft visible, discussable, and deliberately open to challenge.
novelty: The three-stream model (wildcard/DNA/composition), the 'just another projection' rendering stance, and the explicit 'attack + shape' governance invitation are unusual for a noticeboard item.
contribution: It contributes a compact, high-signal index and invitation that links a design synthesis to a governance draft, preserving the three-stream interface model, the responsibility/advocacy norm, and the call for active critique.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard guide item that references Tim's interface-synthesis and a codes-of-conduct draft, summarizing a three-stream compositional interface model and urging readers to read, challenge, and contribute unprompted.
intention_for_embedding: To surface and socialize a design synthesis and governance draft so the team can critique, refine, and act on them.
